122268.explanation: GB! Local first has voted to establish a referendum. We find it important to involve residents in everything.

122272.explanation: GB! Local first thinks it is important to encourage initiatives like this, though financial support is not always necessary; support by helping with subsidies is possible.

122274.explanation: GB! Local first believes we must take care to preserve our natural environment.

122277.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for this. We encourage clubs and associations to take this up per village. GB! Local first believes the municipality can play a connecting role in this.

122279.explanation: This is part of corporate social responsibility, according to GB! Local first. In principle, entrepreneurs arrange this themselves; in exceptional cases, assistance is provided via the entrepreneurs’ fund.

122280.explanation: GB! Local first encourages adding housing on own plots within the established spatial vision. Farmers also deal with regulations regarding adding houses on their plots. No exceptions can be made for specific target groups.

122282.explanation: GB! Local first finds it important to support local media to create connection through local news. A municipal contribution is already made until 2028. After that, additional funding will be needed.

122285.explanation: GB! Local finds it important that sport is affordable and that there is a varied offer. Therefore, we support establishing a sports fund that clubs in need can access. This sports fund is managed and funded by sports associations, the municipality, and other subsidies.

122289.explanation: GB! Local first thinks that at proven unsafe locations, camera surveillance can have a positive effect. Think of the station or the village center of Wolfheze.

122290.explanation: The 'Stay Away from My Body' shelter has just left the municipality. Renkum already has many care institutions; when there is demand, it must be investigated whether there is support and capacity in the villages, says GB! Local first.

122294.explanation: Space for tourism and recreation must also be guaranteed. GB! Local first believes there must remain room for this sector.

122295.explanation: In Renkum, this is also arranged via the Participation Act. GB! Local first believes that we can sometimes be a bit stricter on this.

122298.explanation: Electric driving is the future. GB! Local first thinks it is smart to anticipate this.

122299.explanation: As GB! Local first, we have already achieved results here, but it remains a point of attention. During major road maintenance, the road must be designed as a 30 km/h zone.

122300.explanation: GB!Lokaal op 1 values freedom of choice where possible, but healthcare is overloaded and there is a shortage of workers, so it is not always feasible.

122302.explanation: GB!Lokaal op 1 believes this should be brought back to the national average. We will continue to fight for reasonable rates.

122305.explanation: As long as the social rental housing stock does not grow, we cannot accommodate new status holders, says GB!Lokaal op 1. We are not against housing status holders in general. The balance must be maintained.

122308.explanation: Schools themselves choose to abolish swimming lessons. Parents arrange swimming lessons themselves. If there is no money for this, parents can get a contribution via the Gelrepas. GB!Lokaal op 1 thinks this is a good arrangement!

122310.explanation: GB!Lokaal op 1 does not want any coffeeshops in our villages.

122312.explanation: In some places, high-rise buildings or an additional floor on existing flats can offer a solution to the housing shortage.

122313.explanation: The village centers need a strong impulse to increase liveliness, says GB!Lokaal op 1.

122314.explanation: Oosterbeek deliberately profiles itself as a village center where parking is well organized and entrepreneurs benefit from this.

122317.explanation: GB!Lokaal op 1 sees no reason to enforce more strictly.

122318.explanation: Poverty is increasing and there is a lot of hidden poverty. GB!Lokaal op 1 does not want to lower the threshold.

122319.explanation: GB!Lokaal op 1 wants to build within the built-up area. We must cherish the green surroundings outside the centers. That combination of living environment makes Renkum unique!

122268.explanation: An advisory referendum is one way for residents to have their say. More democracy can also come through citizens' assemblies, participation in planning, contact with your councilors, and of course: elections.

122272.explanation: Sustainable energy does not need subsidies. However, financial support and cooperation from the municipality are needed for planning. GroenLinks-PvdA is happy to provide that.

122274.explanation: Trees are vital. The number of trees has decreased in recent years. That is worrying! GroenLinks-PvdA wants more protection for trees and always replanting.

122277.explanation: Residents can organize a fireworks show themselves if they want. The municipality ensures through permits that it happens safely. There are also other alternatives, such as a light show with lasers.

122279.explanation: No one should be excluded because everyone belongs. Sometimes extra is needed, like a gentler threshold, a wider entrance, or a lift. If entrepreneurs cannot afford that, the municipality looks for subsidies.

122280.explanation: There are already many paved-over spots in the villages. New houses should be built there. The open countryside remains green.

122282.explanation: A free press is necessary for a healthy democracy. There is a lot of nonsense on the internet; people have the right to accurate information.

122285.explanation: Sport connects people and is healthy for everyone.

122289.explanation: Police, enforcement officers, and investigation work well together in Renkum. Camera surveillance is used where necessary.

122290.explanation: Everyone safe, that is the top priority. Those who are unsafe at home can count on help and support. Preferably, we remove a perpetrator from the home so a victim can remain in their own environment. When shelter is needed, it must be available.

122294.explanation: In our beautiful municipality, tourism and recreation belong. People come to enjoy nature and the landscape. That is also good for the economy.

122295.explanation: Participating in society contributes to a better life. We wish that for every resident. Those who need help with that receive it. But sometimes someone is (temporarily) unable to do so. Even then, you have the right to money to live on.

122298.explanation: For a healthy planet, we must abandon polluting fuels. Clean transport is the future, so sufficient places to charge are needed.

122299.explanation: Safety of pedestrians and cyclists comes first. Our villages should be arranged so that an 8-year-old child can just cycle to school or the sports club alone.

122300.explanation: Freedom of choice is a great asset in the Netherlands. If the care offered by the municipality does not suit you, you can use a PGB (personal budget) to purchase care yourself.

122302.explanation: Those with the greatest means carry the heaviest burdens. Municipal tax is a way to collectively pay for all facilities; from schools to elderly care, and from playgrounds to Airborne commemorations.

122305.explanation: New residents of Renkum want to build their lives through learning, working, and integration. This happens fastest with a place of their own. About 10% of social rental homes are for status holders. 20% goes to other groups with urgency.

122308.explanation: Every child a swimming diploma! That can be done in free time. Most people can afford their child's swimming lessons. Those who cannot get a contribution from the municipality.

122310.explanation: When a permit application is submitted, the municipality must assess whether it meets the rules for fair and safe business practices. This is legally regulated.

122312.explanation: More housing can be built by building upwards. For many people, that also has a social function. Of course, not every location is suitable for this, and careful consideration is needed.

122313.explanation: In every village, people should be able to meet. Whether it is a community center, an art center, or a green park with cozy seating.

122314.explanation: A lively shopping area becomes even more attractive if cars are parked on the edges. Then everyone can safely enjoy the space and there is more room for greenery, terraces, and benches.

122317.explanation: Rules apply to everyone. We must ensure that permits can be applied for simply and quickly, without unnecessary rules.

122318.explanation: Getting by on minimum wage is almost impossible. People who work hard and still have to watch every penny deserve our support too.

122319.explanation: The beautiful landscape is the core quality of the municipality of Renkum. New homes should be built where there is already stone. For example, where old offices, industrial sites, and vacant care buildings stand.

122268.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of a referendum. A referendum reduces complex societal issues to a simple yes-or-no choice, losing nuance. Citizen participation is preferred.

122272.explanation: The VVD does not want to oblige residents to take sustainability measures but encourages them to do so voluntarily. A contribution can be an incentive. Good information is also important.

122274.explanation: The municipality of Renkum is already known for its amount of greenery and trees. Residents cherish this. Additional regulations in this area are not needed.

122277.explanation: The costs of this do not outweigh the benefits. We do support initiatives from residents in this area.

122279.explanation: The government sets high demands on entrepreneurs. This can particularly support small entrepreneurs.

122280.explanation: We prefer to build homes on agricultural land rather than solar panels. Initiatives for this must be supported by the municipality. No houses will be built in nature areas.

122282.explanation: There are already obligations to provide a contribution. The municipality of Renkum does not need to provide an additional contribution.

122285.explanation: Sports clubs are responsible for sound financial management. The municipality can offer support in case of difficulties, but this does not have to be a financial contribution.

122289.explanation: Camera surveillance increases the chances of catching offenders and provides monitoring of risk areas, such as Europaplein Renkum, Utrechtseweg Oosterbeek, and Doorwerth shopping center.

122290.explanation: This can be addressed regionally and does not have to be done locally.

122294.explanation: Due to nitrogen and grid congestion, insufficient construction will take place in Renkum in the coming years. However, more housing is needed. We must focus on what is possible.

122295.explanation: The principle is that everyone contributes according to their ability. For those who can no longer work, options such as customized workplaces, meaningful daytime activities, or volunteer work will be explored. Support can be provided for this; those unwilling to work may face benefit reductions.

122300.explanation: Renkum spends a lot on social provisions, about 60% of the budget. It is important for all residents that this is spent efficiently and thoughtfully. The efforts of care professionals, informal care, time, and money must primarily reach those who need it most.

122302.explanation: Renkum has experienced huge increases in property tax (OZB) in recent years. We do not accept increasing local taxes as a means to balance the municipality’s finances. Therefore, we will ensure these levies remain as low as possible.

122305.explanation: The municipality must comply with legal obligations in this regard, but no more than that.

122310.explanation: Where a well-regulated coffeeshop contributes to reducing drug sales and related crime, the VVD may consider allowing it. This is currently not the case in Renkum.

122312.explanation: This is not preferred but can be a solution to reduce the housing shortage. A local approach must be taken, and it must be appropriate for the location.

122313.explanation: Attractive shopping centers strengthen our local economy. They offer a safe and lively environment for residents and entrepreneurs. To combat vacancy, we focus on shopping centers in Oosterbeek and Renkum and a small-scale shopping center in Doorwerth.

122314.explanation: Many visitors appreciate Oosterbeek’s center precisely for its accessibility. A car-free center is not good for the local economy.

122317.explanation: We support developers to speed up procedures. Partners such as ODRA and the Spatial Quality Committee operate from a “yes, provided” attitude instead of “no, unless.”

122318.explanation: National limits must be followed here, and they should not be increased.

122319.explanation: No construction will take place in nature areas, but we do want more opportunities for building on agricultural plots or farmyards. Limits must be maximally utilized to build homes or split plots. We prefer to build homes rather than install solar panels on agricultural land.

122268.explanation: For D66, good citizen representation is important. A referendum is often not a good tool for important and complex questions, as difficult subjects are reduced to yes or no. Therefore, we opt for a citizens' assembly.

122272.explanation: Solving grid congestion is now more important than generating more power. It is not up to local governments to encourage further energy generation. The municipality can coordinate supply and demand but does not provide subsidies.

122274.explanation: This certainly applies to public land. A tree forms its own ecosystem. Especially old trees are very valuable for health and nature. We must take good care of our trees. For every tree cut down, we plant at least one tree back, preferably more.

122277.explanation: As an alternative to private fireworks, a joint celebration is logical in our view. This can be organized by associations with help from the municipality. It offers an alternative moment of joy and tradition to welcome the new year, helping to prevent multiple days of fireworks nuisance.

122279.explanation: We strive for an inclusive society where everyone has access to facilities. Unfortunately, the municipality does not set the rules. Therefore, we do not find it appropriate for the municipality to provide subsidies for this.

122280.explanation: Farms greatly influence the experience of the countryside. Without weighing the public interest, it is not acceptable to build houses on agricultural land arbitrarily. Local authorities must continue to decide where building is allowed.

122282.explanation: It is not the municipalities' role to subsidize the media. Appropriate national policy should be developed for this.

122285.explanation: Every association must be financially independent. Associations are responsible for themselves. The municipality can provide temporary support if solutions are being worked on. The municipality can assist associations with subsidy applications and appropriate volunteer policy.

122289.explanation: It remains important to strike a good balance between privacy and safety. Currently, there is no reason to assume that more camera surveillance makes Renkum safer.

122290.explanation: Renkum does not necessarily have to have its own location. Surrounding cities in the region already provide this. If Renkum proves to be a suitable location for a women's shelter, this can be accommodated.

122294.explanation: The surroundings and facilities of holiday homes are often not suitable for permanent residence. It must fit the location, which can only be assessed case by case.

122295.explanation: D66 Renkum finds participation very important. It reduces the distance to the labor market and also provides meaning. It is not up to municipalities to set rules for reciprocal obligations for benefits. Suitable national policy should be made for this.

122298.explanation: We want the number of public charging points in the municipality to grow in line with the number of electric cars. Not too fast, but not too slow either.

122299.explanation: If it improves safety, 30 km/h is justifiable for specific locations. When we carry out works in public spaces, we always look for ways to improve traffic safety. A general speed limit is not up to the municipality but to the national government.

122300.explanation: As a care provider, you may not discriminate against those who help you. Care providers promise in their oath to assist everyone regardless of background, culture, or belief. This principle should work both ways in our view.

122302.explanation: The property tax (OZB) currently matches the ambitions of our municipality. If OZB is lowered, the municipality can do less for its residents and facilities may have to close.

122305.explanation: Status holders are vulnerable in the housing market. To participate in society as soon as possible, housing is needed. Therefore, they, like other urgent groups, have priority for social rental housing.

122308.explanation: We find it important that everyone learns to swim. However, this is a matter for the national government to decide and finance. We do not want to spend municipal money on this.

122310.explanation: Provided the nuisance is limited and only legal cannabis is sold, we believe this should be allowed.

122312.explanation: If we cannot expand horizontally, we go upwards. Combined with the housing need for small affordable apartments, we consider preserving views less important than creating new homes.

122313.explanation: It is not possible to force where shops and facilities are located. The municipality must ensure facilities are accessible by providing an accessible public space and advocating for good public transport. For example, Heveadorp is too small for a village center.

122314.explanation: The center of Oosterbeek is not suitable to be made car-free due to the Utrechtseweg (Provincial Road). For entrepreneurs and residents, it is important that we ensure the center remains pleasantly accessible to pedestrians, cyclists, and motorists.

122317.explanation: Rules only have value if they are enforced. Therefore, we want strict enforcement of building regulations. We do not want unnecessary rules. Rules should serve the public interest.

122318.explanation: The income limit is set at a fixed percentage of the subsistence minimum. That is appropriate. Lowering this limit means fewer households qualify. This causes problems for low-income households.

122319.explanation: D66 chooses to build within the village boundaries where possible. If that is not enough, we do not exclude new construction outside those boundaries. But only if it is done carefully, includes nature, and truly contributes to the housing need.

122268.explanation: CDA wants as much participation from residents as possible, involving them early in the decision-making process. This way the opinions of residents are considered, without cumbersome and costly referenda that often have low turnout.

122272.explanation: Sustainability: CDA supports residents and guides them to the appropriate offices for advice and funding (subsidies) but not at the expense of the municipality.

122274.explanation: Preservation of green environment depends further on the surroundings if there is limited green space.

122277.explanation: With six villages, it is difficult to organize. Better to connect with a large neighboring municipality. Costs are too high to do this properly.

122279.explanation: We can help by directing entrepreneurs to regional offices and funds, but as a municipality we do not have funds available for this except for public spaces.

122280.explanation: Depends on the environment vision and environmental plan.

122282.explanation: Reaching as many people as possible is fine, but the current budget is sufficient.

122285.explanation: Depends on the financial management of the respective association. (VP H3.3 associations).

122290.explanation: Can also be arranged at the regional level. In Renkum, one was recently closed.

122294.explanation: Within the framework of housing shortage and for a limited period of, for example, 15 years. After that, we will see whether permanent status is desirable at that specific location.

122295.explanation: We do aim to let these people participate (Ch. 5). For those who are capable, it is a good form of reintegration.

122298.explanation: In line with the energy transition and sustainability (VP Ch. 2.1 and 2.3).

122299.explanation: This is a key point from the CDA election program 2022 – 2026. It has been realized in the Mobility Vision 2040.

122300.explanation: Can be done via PGB (VP Ch. 5.3).

122302.explanation: Frugality and solid finances (max indexed to CPI) can lead to a stable property tax (OZB). Inferred from text Ch. 7 p 19.

122308.explanation: Subsidize swimming lessons for children of parents with a Gelrepas. So no school swimming for all children whose parents can afford it (currently an A-B diploma costs about €1000 with commercial providers).

122310.explanation: Does not fit in our municipality; go to, for example, Arnhem, where there are sufficient points of sale.

122312.explanation: Provided it fits within the environmental vision.

122313.explanation: We support all villages (Ch. 6). We already have centers in Renkum, Oosterbeek, Doorwerth (partly), Heelsum (limited). Wolfheze and Heveadorp lack the scale, but with new construction near ProPersona in Wolfheze, that is a realistic scenario.

122314.explanation: We are in favor of entrepreneurs and entrepreneurship (Ch. 4). Accessibility and livability for Oosterbeek center is essential for all residents.

122317.explanation: The permit application must be simplified. This will reduce illegal construction. Especially large project developers must be stricter controlled when placing objects or large constructions without permits than are authorized.

122318.explanation: The current policy suffices and aligns with our VP. Lowering this limit (120%) is not on the table.

122319.explanation: Infill development is preferred, but in certain situations it must be possible to build outside.

122268.explanation: As Party Renkumse Dorpen, we made sure a referendum regulation was introduced. Unfortunately, most parties do not want residents to have the initiative. The council decides if it happens.

122272.explanation: It is not about obligation, at most it can be done based on a regulation.

122274.explanation: Renkum has many forests and natural greenery, but many trees disappear in the residential areas. We support the possibility of exchanging replanting obligations. The location of the new tree can be arranged in consultation with the municipality. New felling policy is needed.

122277.explanation: It is not a matter of obligation; rather consider if an Orange Association can play a role.

122279.explanation: For non-municipal buildings, the owner is responsible and such matters should not be forced. But a good entrepreneur wants to be customer-friendly and will seriously consider this.

122280.explanation: Through zoning plans and the elaboration of an environmental plan, the municipality has determined some options in consultation with residents.

122282.explanation: Then you should be able to influence their policy, and the local government should not interfere in that.

122285.explanation: That can also be done by performing services like leaf clearing. You can try to have people on social assistance do volunteer work (e.g., in hospitality) to gain work experience.

122289.explanation: In principle, you should not want camera surveillance. It can be desirable in occasional circumstances. Think of construction sites!

122290.explanation: Such a provision is always desirable. "Safe at Home" as an additional facility (information, etc.) is fine, but not sufficient.

122294.explanation: Unfortunately, we have done that for a holiday park in Wolfheze. A holiday home as a second residence does not fit in the current time of housing scarcity. A holiday home should in principle be used as such.

122295.explanation: We are in favor of structurally encouraging those people to do so. But on a voluntary basis.

122300.explanation: You have to take it into account as much as possible. You cannot guarantee it in advance.

122302.explanation: In Renkum, the increase in property values generates additional property tax revenue. We want to determine in advance which income is needed and adjust the rates accordingly, so lower them.

122305.explanation: We already have a major housing shortage in the municipality of Renkum. There are families with sons/daughters in their 30s who have been waiting for years for affordable housing.

122308.explanation: School swimming for two school years contributes to children's health and is good for social interaction. Attention to swimming as a skill, sport (polo, competitive swimming), lifesaving swimming, and synchronized swimming.

122310.explanation: The PRD does not have a need for that. It is inviting, attracts people from elsewhere, and can cause nuisances.

122312.explanation: What is high? We want to counter horizon pollution.

122313.explanation: The PRD finds a central meeting place with shops and hospitality important. Oosterbeek can be redesigned, Wolfheze should get a square. Attention to the proper scale is also needed for Heveadorp.

122314.explanation: We support an underground parking garage beneath the market square in Oosterbeek and want to retain blue zones where necessary.

122317.explanation: It is about assessing when a permit is required or not. That is stated in the zoning plan. General information provision should be done structurally.

122318.explanation: First, it must be clear which poverty threshold we use, the number of people involved, which schemes those people already benefit from, and how we reach that target group. We differentiate between receiving assistance (types of aid), no income politics.

122319.explanation: That is not the PRD’s preference. But what is a village boundary? Traffic built-up area sign or a continuous residential area with overflow into nature/forests? We choose additional housing in the villages but not a fully built-up village.

122268.explanation: Residents should always be able to express what they want. Whether they want an expensive town hall or not.

122272.explanation: Provided this does not lead to a villa subsidy.

122274.explanation: The municipality of Renkum is one of the greenest municipalities in the Netherlands and that is something to be proud of. Nature is essential for our municipality and everyone can enjoy it. In short, preserve and expand where possible.

122277.explanation: The money that would go to this fireworks show can better be invested in care and education.

122279.explanation: The SP Renkum wants everyone to be able to participate in society.

122280.explanation: We support housing construction but do not want uncontrolled proliferation of houses.

122282.explanation: Local broadcasters must be sufficiently supported to perform their role in the democratic process.

122285.explanation: Sports must remain accessible to every resident.

122289.explanation: The SP is against camera surveillance. Installing cameras creates a false sense of security and does not solve the problems.

122290.explanation: SP Renkum wants the municipality of Renkum to work intensively with neighboring municipalities and focus on preventive policy.

122294.explanation: Given the fact that we are in a housing crisis, SP Renkum accepts this emergency solution for now. This must not lead to any degree of nuisance.

122295.explanation: The SP wants people on social assistance to flow into regular work. Where necessary, they make use of the jobs agreement.

122298.explanation: Electric driving is no longer actively stimulated by the government and will not increase as quickly anymore.

122299.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount here and where necessary we lower speed to protect residents from accidents.

122300.explanation: SP Renkum believes that people who need care should be able to choose who provides their care. Due to labor market shortages, the SP realizes this is not always possible, but where it is, it must be allowed.

122302.explanation: The property tax can be reduced provided this is compensated by charging higher incomes and polluters more in the municipality of Renkum. SP Renkum calls for higher contributions from larger businesses in the municipality.

122305.explanation: Refugees are welcome and can be temporarily accommodated without problem. Refugees with a status are seen by the SP as equals compared to our own residents. The SP mainly wants more social housing to be built to solve this problem.

122308.explanation: The SP wants every child to be able to swim.

122310.explanation: At this moment, there are enough options in our neighboring municipalities to meet this need.

122312.explanation: The SP wants to preserve nature and that may mean housing will be built higher. However, this must be done in consultation with the residents of the municipality of Renkum.

122313.explanation: The SP retains all village centers currently in existence.

122314.explanation: The center of Oosterbeek must remain accessible and safe for our residents and entrepreneurs. Where possible, the SP looks to make it low-traffic.

122317.explanation: The SP believes the law is the same for everyone. Enforcement is necessary in this regard.

122318.explanation: The SP wants this to remain the same or be increased. Where necessary, tailored solutions must be provided. People should not fall into a poverty trap. With an increase, more people can receive help.

122319.explanation: The nature in Renkum must be protected and there are enough opportunities within the village cores to create more housing.

122268.explanation: We must submit important decisions to the residents. We are talking about fundamental questions here.

122272.explanation: We support sustainable energy, especially on roofs of homes and buildings. There are already schemes for this at the national level. We should not bring these tasks and associated financial obligations down to the municipal level.

122274.explanation: This is a good idea. In our municipality, we have enough space within and outside the built-up area to do this. Trees absorb CO2 and purify the air we breathe.

122277.explanation: If the fireworks ban goes ahead, we definitely need to have an alternative. We continue to support safe consumer fireworks use; illegal fireworks must be tackled severely.

122279.explanation: If we can help an entrepreneur realize this, for example through permitting, we should do so. However, we should not want to bear the financial costs ourselves.

122280.explanation: We are thinking of building on the designated building plot or adjoining it. If this can be designated as residential, we should certainly consider it.

122282.explanation: We do not have a local broadcaster in our municipality.

122285.explanation: We must definitely support these associations. However, we must not lose sight of their utility and necessity.

122289.explanation: Cameras may only be installed in unsafe places provided this is clearly indicated.

122290.explanation: We had the Paula Foundation, which has moved to Arnhem. We are open to initiatives in this area.

122294.explanation: We lack housing; this could be a good solution to the housing shortage.

122298.explanation: Where possible, this should definitely be done, provided enough parking spaces remain for regular cars.

122300.explanation: All care provided by us as a municipality meets national standards and is equal for everyone.

122302.explanation: We unjustly increased these last year (2025) by 40%, but this year (2026) there is a surplus. We want to be a reliable government.

122305.explanation: We do not have enough living space; our own residents take priority. We want to inform the national government that we cannot meet housing obligations for status holders.

122308.explanation: We live along the Neder Rijn, so every child must have the basic swimming safety diploma in our municipality.

122310.explanation: This is legal, and if there is demand in our municipality, we legally cannot prohibit it. There must simply be no nuisance caused.

122312.explanation: We support a maximum building height of 4 floors including the ground floor. This fits the village character of our municipality.

122313.explanation: All villages in the municipality already have a village center. We want to continue investing in them to maintain livability.

122314.explanation: Pedestrians, cyclists, and cars have coexisted for years.

122317.explanation: If you do not follow the rules, enforcement must take place.

122318.explanation: Depending on which municipal scheme is involved, we must look specifically at who falls below the poverty line and who does not.

122319.explanation: Where possible, we must definitely do this to solve the housing crisis in our municipality for our residents. We will keep an eye on preserving our green living environment.

statements.title: The municipality should allow new construction outside the existing village boundaries.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Renkum should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Oosterbeek's town centre should become car-free.
The residents of Renkum should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Renkum moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
More high-rise apartment buildings should be allowed in Renkum.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should lower the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
Renkum should have a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Building without a permit should be tackled more strictly in Renkum.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

titleParts.text: moet in Renkum strenger worden aangepakt.
